Machine Learning 101

Steps Count for Fitness Journey

Linear Regression
Author

Nikhita Purohit

Published

March 17, 2026

[ Discarded because of too many errors]

Objective: The motivation for gathering this dataset was personal, with the goal of answering a simple question: “Impact of Daily Step Count on Weight Loss Journey?” # #

  1. Setting up R Packages
library(readr)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(ggformula)
library(janitor)
library(naniar)
library(visdat)
  1. Reading Data
step <- readr::read_csv("../../data/Steps Export_ 3-17-21to9-23-22.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
  1. Examining and Cleaning Data
dim(step)
[1] 526   8
str(step)
spc_tbl_ [526 × 8] (S3: spec_tbl_df/tbl_df/tbl/data.frame)
 $ Date                        : chr [1:526] "3/17/2021" "3/18/2021" "3/19/2021" "3/20/2021" ...
 $ Step Count                  : num [1:526] 1500 3165 1494 1808 2667 ...
 $ Distance Travelled          : chr [1:526] "1.02 km" "2.23 km" "1.08 km" "1.27 km" ...
 $ Active Time                 : chr [1:526] "23:14 min" "44:56 min" "24:51 min" "30:04 min" ...
 $ Flights Climbed             : num [1:526] 3 2 0 1 3 1 2 2 1 1 ...
 $ Goal                        : num [1:526] 10000 10000 10000 10000 10000 10000 10000 10000 10000 10000 ...
 $ Percentage of Goal Completed: chr [1:526] "15%" "32%" "15%" "18%" ...
 $ Calories                    : num [1:526] 64 134 68 81 103 105 161 36 113 75 ...
 - attr(*, "spec")=
  .. cols(
  ..   Date = col_character(),
  ..   `Step Count` = col_double(),
  ..   `Distance Travelled` = col_character(),
  ..   `Active Time` = col_character(),
  ..   `Flights Climbed` = col_double(),
  ..   Goal = col_double(),
  ..   `Percentage of Goal Completed` = col_character(),
  ..   Calories = col_double()
  .. )
 - attr(*, "problems")=<externalptr> 
head(step)
# A tibble: 6 × 8
  Date   `Step Count` `Distance Travelled` `Active Time` `Flights Climbed`  Goal
  <chr>         <dbl> <chr>                <chr>                     <dbl> <dbl>
1 3/17/…         1500 1.02 km              23:14 min                     3 10000
2 3/18/…         3165 2.23 km              44:56 min                     2 10000
3 3/19/…         1494 1.08 km              24:51 min                     0 10000
4 3/20/…         1808 1.27 km              30:04 min                     1 10000
5 3/21/…         2667 1.78 km              30:57 min                     3 10000
6 3/22/…         2368 1.69 km              37:25 min                     1 10000
# ℹ 2 more variables: `Percentage of Goal Completed` <chr>, Calories <dbl>
tail(step)
# A tibble: 6 × 8
  Date   `Step Count` `Distance Travelled` `Active Time` `Flights Climbed`  Goal
  <chr>         <dbl> <chr>                <chr>                     <dbl> <dbl>
1 9/18/…         7415 5.61 km              1:20 hr                       0  3500
2 9/19/…         1665 1.14 km              26:09 min                     1  3500
3 9/20/…         3676 2.62 km              52:05 min                     0  3500
4 9/21/…          444 0.32 km              7:25 min                      0  3500
5 9/22/…          506 0.35 km              8:19 min                      0  3500
6 9/23/…          414 0.29 km              6:51 min                      0 10000
# ℹ 2 more variables: `Percentage of Goal Completed` <chr>, Calories <dbl>
names(step)
[1] "Date"                         "Step Count"                  
[3] "Distance Travelled"           "Active Time"                 
[5] "Flights Climbed"              "Goal"                        
[7] "Percentage of Goal Completed" "Calories"                    
visdat::vis_dat(step, sort_type = TRUE)
step_modified <- step %>% tidyr::drop_na()
visdat::vis_dat(step_modified, sort_type = TRUE)
step_modified <- step %>%
  dplyr::mutate(across(where(is.character), as.factor)) %>% 
  relocate(where(is.factor))
glimpse(step_modified)
Rows: 526
Columns: 8
$ Date                           <fct> 3/17/2021, 3/18/2021, 3/19/2021, 3/20/2…
$ `Distance Travelled`           <fct> 1.02 km, 2.23 km, 1.08 km, 1.27 km, 1.7…
$ `Active Time`                  <fct> 23:14 min, 44:56 min, 24:51 min, 30:04 …
$ `Percentage of Goal Completed` <fct> 15%, 32%, 15%, 18%, 27%, 24%, 36%, 8%, …
$ `Step Count`                   <dbl> 1500, 3165, 1494, 1808, 2667, 2368, 359…
$ `Flights Climbed`              <dbl> 3, 2, 0, 1, 3, 1, 2, 2, 1, 1, 0, 2, 2, …
$ Goal                           <dbl> 10000, 10000, 10000, 10000, 10000, 1000…
$ Calories                       <dbl> 64, 134, 68, 81, 103, 105, 161, 36, 113…
step_modified %>%
  DT::datatable(
    style = "default",
    caption = htmltools::tags$caption(
      style = "caption-side: top; text-align: left; color: black; font-size: 100%;", "Steps Count for Fitness Journey Dataset (Clean)"
    ),
    options = list(pageLength = 10, autoWidth = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  DT::formatStyle(
    columns = names(step_modified),
    fontFamily = "Arial",
    fontSize = "12px",
  )

  1. Data Dictionary

Quantitative Data

  1. Step Count (int): The total number of steps taken during the day.
  2. Distance Travelled (dbl): The total distance covered (in km).
  3. Active Time (dbl): The total amount of time spent in physical activity (in minutes).
  4. Flights Climbed (int): The number of floors or flights of stairs ascended.
  5. Goal (int): The daily target number of steps set by the user.
  6. Percentage of Goal Completed (dbl): The progress toward the daily step goal, expressed as a percentage.
  7. Calories (dbl): The total amount of energy expended (in kcal).

Qualitative Data

  1. Date (date): The specific calendar day the data was recorded.
summary(step)
     Date             Step Count    Distance Travelled Active Time       
 Length:526         Min.   :    0   Length:526         Length:526        
 Class :character   1st Qu.: 1297   Class :character   Class :character  
 Mode  :character   Median : 2366   Mode  :character   Mode  :character  
                    Mean   : 3174                                        
                    3rd Qu.: 4370                                        
                    Max.   :12432                                        
 Flights Climbed        Goal       Percentage of Goal Completed    Calories    
 Min.   : 0.0000   Min.   : 3500   Length:526                   Min.   :  0.0  
 1st Qu.: 0.0000   1st Qu.: 3500   Class :character             1st Qu.: 60.0  
 Median : 0.0000   Median : 3500   Mode  :character             Median :108.0  
 Mean   : 0.7243   Mean   : 4229                                Mean   :142.9  
 3rd Qu.: 1.0000   3rd Qu.: 3500                                3rd Qu.:191.0  
 Max.   :10.0000   Max.   :10000                                Max.   :595.0  
# Fixing the data set because three columns have units in their records

step_modified <- step_modified %>%
  mutate(
    `Active Time` = case_when(
      grepl("hr", `Active Time`) ~ {
        time <- gsub(" hr", "", `Active Time`)
        parts <- strsplit(time, ":")
        sapply(parts, function(x) {
          if(length(x) == 2) {
            as.numeric(x[1]) * 60 + as.numeric(x[2])
          } else {
            NA_real_
          }
        })
      },
      grepl("min", `Active Time`) ~ {
        time <- gsub(" min", "", `Active Time`)
        parts <- strsplit(time, ":")
        sapply(parts, function(x) {
          if(length(x) == 2) {
            as.numeric(x[1]) + as.numeric(x[2]) / 60
          } else {
            NA_real_
          }
        })
      }
    )
  )
Warning: There were 526 warnings in `mutate()`.
The first warning was:
ℹ In argument: `Active Time = case_when(...)`.
Caused by warning in `FUN()`:
! NAs introduced by coercion
ℹ Run `dplyr::last_dplyr_warnings()` to see the 525 remaining warnings.
step_modified <- step_modified %>%
  mutate(`Distance Travelled` =
           as.numeric(as.character(`Distance Travelled`)))
Warning: There was 1 warning in `mutate()`.
ℹ In argument: `Distance Travelled = as.numeric(as.character(`Distance
  Travelled`))`.
Caused by warning:
! NAs introduced by coercion
  1. Graphs
step_modified %>%
  gf_histogram(~ `Step Count`,
               fill = "cyan4",
               bins = 20) %>%
  gf_labs(title = "Step Count",
          x = "Count",
          y = "Number of People")
step_modified %>%
  gf_histogram(~ `Distance Travelled`,
               fill = "orange",
               data = step_modified) %>%
  gf_labs(title = "Distance Travelled",
          x = "Distance (km)",
          y = "Number of People")
`stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value `binwidth`.
Warning: Removed 526 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_bin()`).
step_modified %>%
  gf_histogram(~ `Active Time`,
               fill = "blue",
               bins = 20) %>%
  gf_labs(title = "Active Time",
          x = "Time (min)",
          y = "Number of People")
step_modified %>%
  gf_histogram(~ `Flights Climbed`,
               fill = "pink",
               bins = 20) %>%
  gf_labs(title = "Flights Climbed",
          x = "Count",
          y = "Number of People")
step_modified %>%
  gf_histogram(~ `Goal`,
               fill = "lightgreen",
               bins = 20) %>%
  gf_labs(title = "Goal",
          x = "Number of Steps",
          y = "Number of People")
# step_modified %>%
#   gf_histogram(~ `Percentage of Goal Completed`,
#                fill = "maroon",
#                bins = 20) %>%
#   gf_labs(title = "Percentage of Goal Completed",
#           x = "Number (in %)",
#           y = "Number of People")

step_modified %>%
  gf_histogram(~ Calories,
               fill = "purple",
               bins = 20) %>%
  gf_labs(title = "Calories",
          x = "Calories (in kcal)",
          y = "Number of People")
